channel.title: Michael LaPlante's Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on security, engineering, and building things.

item.title: Implementing Zero Trust in Multi-Cloud Environments: Practical Strategies and Pitfalls
item.description: The move to multi-cloud is accelerating. Whether it's AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, or a combination, organizations are spreading workloads across platfor...

item.title: Navigating AI-Powered Threats: Practical Strategies for Securing GenAI Tools in Cloud Environments
item.description: AI is changing the security landscape at an unprecedented pace. Generative AI (GenAI) tools are now part of many cloud workflows, promising efficiency...

item.title: Using AI LLMs to Streamline Security Vulnerability Updates
item.description: Security vulnerabilities are a moving target. Every week, new threats emerge and old software dependencies get patched. For security and operations te...

item.title: What Happens When You Let AI Drive Your Dev Workflow for a Day
item.description: I spent a Saturday building a blog, automating deployments, fixing dark mode bugs, and writing this post — all through conversation with Claude Code.

item.title: Building a Blog Subsection in One Session with Astro and Claude
item.description: How I went from zero to a fully functional blog subsection — designed, built, and styled — in a single AI-assisted development session.

item.title: Your Data Is the Product: Privacy in the Age of AI Tools
item.description: AI tools are everywhere — and so is your data. Shadow AI, regulatory whiplash, and the erosion of data boundaries are redefining what privacy means in 2026.

item.title: Ditching DeployHQ: Free FTP Deployments with GitHub Actions
item.description: How I replaced a paid deployment service with a free GitHub Actions workflow that builds my Astro blog and deploys to FTP in 20 seconds.

item.title: Back to Full Sail: Speaking at Hall of Fame Week 2026
item.description: Returning to my alma mater as a Hall of Fame inductee to give two presentations and mentor the next generation of tech professionals.

item.title: When Your Vulnerability Scanner Becomes the Vulnerability: Lessons from the Trivy Supply Chain Attack
item.description: A vulnerability scanner trusted by millions got backdoored — twice in one month. Here's what happened with Trivy and what it means for everyone running security tools in CI/CD.

item.title: Hello World: Launching the Blog
item.description: A first post to kick off the blog — built with Astro, integrated into my existing resume site.
